messages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\stadmin\Desktop\Training Task\Shared Next JS\Intern-Management-System\src\app\dashboard\page.tsx:30:9
  28 |       try {
  29 |         const payload = JSON.parse(atob(token.split(".")[1]));
> 30 |         setAuthData({
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  31 |           role: payload.role || "intern",
  32 |           userId: payload.userId || "",
  33 |           departmentId: payload.departmentId || null,
'error' is assigned a value but never used.

The 'departments' logical expression could make the dependencies of useMemo Hook (at line 42) change on every render. Move it inside the useMemo callback. Alternatively, wrap the initialization of 'departments' in its own useMemo() Hook.
'error' is assigned a value but never used.
'Department' is defined but never used.
'error' is assigned a value but never used.

The 'interns' logical expression could make the dependencies of useMemo Hook (at line 109) change on every render. Move it inside the useMemo callback. Alternatively, wrap the initialization of 'interns' in its own useMemo() Hook.
Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

suppressedMessages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\stadmin\Desktop\Training Task\Shared Next JS\Intern-Management-System\src\app\settings\page.tsx:33:11
  31 |           const payload = JSON.parse(atob(token.split('.')[1]));
  32 |           // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/set-state-in-effect
> 33 |           setRole(payload.role || "intern");
     |           ^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  34 |           // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/set-state-in-effect
  35 |           setEmail(payload.email || "user@example.com");
  36 |           // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/set-state-in-effect
